entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: Sorghum bicolor (L.) Moench, the crop being studied for resistance to Striga

entities.name: STRIGA HERMONTHICA
entities.description: A parasitic flowering weed that acts as a major biotic constraint to sorghum production

entities.name: SUB-SAHARAN AFRICA
entities.description: The region where Striga is a major constraint to sorghum production

entities.name: AGAR-GEL ASSAY
entities.description: A laboratory assay used to evaluate the stimulation of Striga hermonthica seed germination

entities.name: F1 DIALLEL
entities.description: A breeding design involving crosses between nine sorghum cultivars and inbred lines

entities.name: MALI
entities.description: A country where Striga populations were sourced and pot trials were conducted

entities.name: NIGER
entities.description: A country where Striga populations were sourced and pot trials were conducted

entities.name: POT TRIALS
entities.description: Experiments conducted in pots to observe striga plant emergence

entities.name: STRIGA PLANT EMERGENCE
entities.description: The observation and measurement of striga plants emerging from the soil

entities.name: GERMINATION DISTANCE
entities.description: A measure used in the agar-gel assay to evaluate seed germination stimulation

entities.name: NUMBER OF EMERGED STRIGA
entities.description: The count of striga plants that emerged in pot trials

entities.name: GENERAL COMBINING ABILITY (GCA)
entities.description: The average performance of a parent in a series of crosses

entities.name: SPECIFIC COMBINING ABILITY (SCA)
entities.description: The deviation of a hybrid's performance from the expected value based on GCA

entities.name: BROAD-SENSE HERITABILITIES
entities.description: A measure of the proportion of phenotypic variance due to genetic factors

entities.name: STRIGA RESISTANCE
entities.description: The ability of sorghum to resist infestation by Striga

entities.name: LOW STIMULANT PRODUCTION
entities.description: A specific resistance mechanism where the crop produces fewer stimulants for Striga germination

entities.name: RESISTANCE MECHANISMS
entities.description: Various biological ways sorghum resists Striga infestation
